title: Spring and well protection perimeter
summary: Protects natural water points from runoff, animals, waste, crowd damage, and dirty containers.
content: Spring and well protection perimeter is a practical offline routine for source protection. Mark an inner clean zone where no washing, animals, toilets, trash, fuel, or bathing are allowed. Map uphill runoff, latrines, animal paths, flooded areas, roads, and chemical storage. Use a queue and clean-container rule so dirty vessels are not dipped into the source. Inspect after rain, earthquake, wildfire, crowd use, or animal intrusion. Close or downgrade the source if contamination is suspected. The goal is to make the action repeatable by a tired person, visible to helpers, and safe enough to stop when official help or a safer option appears.
materials: Sign. Context: “Spring and well protection perimeter”.

steps: Mark an inner clean zone where no washing, animals, toilets, trash, fuel, or bathing are allowed.

Use a queue and clean-container rule so dirty vessels are not dipped into the source.
Inspect after rain, earthquake, wildfire, crowd use, or animal intrusion.
Close or downgrade the source if contamination is suspected.
warnings: Use Spring and well protection perimeter as practical support, not as a replacement for official guidance, trained help, or evacuation when needed.

Filtering alone does not make chemically contaminated, fuel-contaminated, sewage-contaminated, or floodwater safe. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Spring and well protection perimeter”.
common_mistakes: Starting from memory instead of written notes while carrying out “Spring and well protection perimeter”
Letting one tired person control the whole process while carrying out “Spring and well protection perimeter”
Ignoring the review time while carrying out “Spring and well protection perimeter”
alternatives: Use a wall board when many people are involved as a fallback for “Spring and well protection perimeter”
Use pocket cards when movement is required as a fallback for “Spring and well protection perimeter”
failure_signs: People disagree about the facts, the plan is not being followed, or the situation worsens after the first review during “Spring and well protection perimeter”
when_not_to_use: Do not use Spring and well protection perimeter to delay emergency care, evacuation, fire response, or official instructions.
short_term: Write the first safe action, responsible person, materials, and next review time. This note applies specifically to Spring and well protection perimeter.
long_term: Convert the routine into a repeatable checklist with handover notes and backups. This note applies specifically to Spring and well protection perimeter.
if_method_fails: Reduce complexity, stop unsafe work, and switch to the safest available fallback. This note applies specifically to Spring and well protection perimeter.
environment_notes: Adapt the routine to heat, cold, crowding, darkness, limited water, and local hazards. This note applies specifically to Spring and well protection perimeter.
